name: Aelyn Kantoro
whyHere: You were sent, or you were seeded, or you simply wanted to see something finish inside a single lifetime.

inventory.name: Traveler's Pack
inventory.description: A worn canvas pack with good straps — bedroll, flint, mess kit, fifty small comforts of the road.

chronicle: Aelyn left the Heartroot with Caelum Kantoro's name, the kept thread settled in her sternum, and Branch's word — family — fresh in her journal. The deep grain quest's receiving is complete; she faces northeast as the first evening of her new life begins.

turns.player: Use Lifesense — trace the thread outward from your sternum and see how far it can reach.
turns.summary: Aelyn steps outside the Heartroot at evening and reaches through Lifesense to trace the kept thread — it leans northeast, steady and real, the other end a living person still breathing somewhere beyond the city's canopy.
turns.narration:
You step out through the Heartroot's living door into the cool evening air, and the city breathes around you — walls still pale with stored afternoon warmth, the path under your feet worn root-smooth by four centuries of unhurried feet. Branch walks beside you, its root-toes re-gripping the ground with each slow step, buds at its shoulder catching the last amber light.

You close your eyes and reach inward — not down into the deep grain this time, but across. Lifesense opens the way it always does: the green pulse of Branch beside you, the slow dreaming warmth of the city walls, the flicker-scatter of small birds settling into the outer canopy. And then, underneath all of it, the kept thread. It sits at the base of your sternum, warm and specific, and when you extend your attention toward it the way you'd extend your hand to feel which direction a draft comes from, it answers. Not with a picture. Not with words. With a lean.

Northeast. Steady. Not faint — the thread has weight, real weight, the kind that means the other end is not a memory but a person still breathing. Your range with Lifesense reaches only to the buildings nearby, but the thread's orientation doesn't waver at the edge of that reach. It continues, past the city's canopy, past the treeline where the early-spring melt has the eastern slopes still silver with snowmelt and new mud. Wherever Caelum Kantoro is, he is beyond your sight. But the thread knows him, and it is not confused about direction.

Branch stops walking and looks where you are looking. A long pause, in the way that Branch pauses when it has decided on a word.

"Far," it says, one syllable, with the particular precision it gives to distances it means seriously.

turns.player: Go back inside and find Maren — ask her what lies northeast that a person might go to, and whether she knew Caelum Kantoro had any destination when he left thirty years ago.
turns.summary: Aelyn returned to the entrance hall and asked Maren what lies northeast — and what Caelum Kantoro said when he left. Maren confirmed he said nothing of a destination, only walked; what lies northeast past the Quickwood's margin is the Hollow March and, beyond it, the Ashwarden lands.
turns.narration:
You turn back from the northeast and tell Branch you'll be a few minutes. It dips its upper branches once — fine — and settles against the root-smooth wall to wait, buds half-open in the cooling air.

Inside, the entrance hall is warm with the day's stored light, and Maren is where she has been all day: standing near the sapling with the particular stillness of someone who has decided this is where she belongs tonight. She looks up when you come back through the door, reads your face, and nods — as though she expected this. 'You want to know where northeast takes you,' she says, before you've said anything.

You ask her plainly: what lies northeast from here that a person might go to? And did Caelum Kantoro say anything about a destination when he left, thirty years ago?

She is quiet for a moment, and in that moment the sapling stirs faintly — a small lean, no more. 'He said nothing of a destination,' Maren says at last. 'He thanked the Root. He thanked me, though I was young then and not yet Tender. He stood in this doorway the way you're standing now and looked northeast. Then he walked.' She pauses, and something careful moves through her expression. 'What lies that way — past the Quickwood's margin, past the Hollow March where growth stops — is the Pale. The Ashwarden lands. Their cities of tended endings.' She meets your eyes. 'We do not go there. But the thread leads, and you must go where it leads. Those are different things.'

turns.player: Ask Maren directly: does she think Caelum is in the Ashwarden lands — and what would a man steeped in the deep-woven frequency want there, at the pole of endings?
turns.summary: Maren named her belief plainly: Caelum went to the Ashwarden lands because the Rootkin would have pressed him to simplify himself, and the Ashwardens work with what a thing actually is. She doesn't know what he gave them in return, but believes Aelyn will recognize it when she finds it.
turns.narration:
Maren is quiet for a moment after you ask it plainly. Not the quiet of someone who doesn't know — the quiet of someone choosing which true thing to say first.

'Yes,' she says. 'I think he went there.' She folds her hands together, a gesture you've seen her use before when she's being careful. 'A man carrying the deep-woven frequency — both sources alive in him, the green and the other — the Ashwarden lands are one of the few places that would not try to simplify him. The Rootkin would have kept asking him to lean further into the green. Here.' She tips her head, meaning the Heartroot itself. 'We would have done that too. But the Ashwardens work with what a thing actually is. Not what it ought to be.' She pauses. 'That is either what he needed, or what he feared he needed. I could not tell you which, because I was young when he left and I did not yet know how to ask the right questions.'

The sapling leans a fraction toward her as she speaks — not toward you, toward her, as if it recognizes the weight she is carrying in the telling. Maren notices and rests two fingers lightly on its nearest branch without looking down.

'What I can tell you,' she adds, 'is that the Ashwardens do not take people in out of kindness. If he stayed among them, he gave them something in return. What a deep-woven man could give them —' She stops. Considers. 'I don't know. But I think you would recognize it when you found it.'

turns.player: Thank Maren and step outside to Branch — you have enough. Write what you know in your journal before the evening takes it, then decide your next move.
turns.summary: Aelyn thanked Maren, acknowledged the sapling, and settled outside with Branch to write what she knows — Caelum Kantoro, northeast, the Ashwarden lands — before the evening took it. The thread at her sternum confirmed his name as she wrote it.
turns.narration:
You thank Maren — not with the formal Rootkin phrase you've been hearing around the city, but plainly, the way you'd thank someone who gave you something they didn't have to. She accepts it with a small nod, fingers lifting briefly from the sapling's branch and then returning. The sapling leans a fraction in your direction as you turn toward the door, and you pause long enough to acknowledge it — a hand raised, not a touch, because you know better than to touch without invitation now. Its warmth in your Lifesense brightens and settles.

Outside, Branch is where you left it — root-toes dug into the path, buds open to the evening air. It looks up at you with the patient attention of something that has no concept of how long it waited. The Heartroot's outer walls hold the last copper of the daylight, and the city around you is shifting into its evening rhythms: a Rootkin woman carrying something woven past without looking up, two others murmuring in the slow, deliberate cadence you've come to recognize as meaningful conversation that will conclude sometime next week.

You find a place where the root-wall curves outward into a low natural seat and sit with your back against the warm wood. The pack comes off your shoulders; the journal comes out. You write what you know in the order you know it, because that's how your mother taught you: first the facts, then the shape they make. *Caelum Kantoro. Father. Alive — northeast.* The kept thread hums faintly at your sternum as you write his name, as if confirming it. *Ashwarden lands — past the Quickwood's margin and the Hollow March. Maren believes he went where something deep-woven would not be asked to be less than it is. What he gave them in return: unknown.* You press the pen still for a moment. *I will recognize it when I find it.* That is the shape the facts make. You write it, and close the journal.

Branch is watching you in the way it has — unhurried, as though watching you breathe is already interesting. The evening deepens around you both. Northeast: the Quickwood first, then the Hollow March, and somewhere beyond it, your father — living, woven, thirty years gone into a land of endings. The thread at your sternum is steady.

choices.label: Ask Branch what it knows of the road northeast — it has been here longer than you; it may know the shape of the Quickwood's margin.
Use Lifesense — turn it northeast again, not to reach the thread, but to read the city's edge: what lives there, what doesn't, and where the Quickwood begins.
Go back inside to Maren — one more question before you leave: does the Hollow March have a safe passage, or will you need a guide?
Rest here for the night — the Heartroot is safe, the thread is steady, and you leave better for sleeping. Northeast in the morning.

scene.setting: Outside the Heartroot, evening — the outer root-wall curved into a low natural seat where Aelyn sits with her journal; the Heartroot's walls copper-warm with last light, the city settling into evening pace around her.

npcsPresent.name: Branch
npcsPresent.state: Root-toes dug into the path beside Aelyn's seat, buds open, watching her with patient attention.

objects: Aelyn's journal (open, just written in — Caelum Kantoro's name, the northeast thread, the Ashwarden lands)
Traveler's Pack (set down beside her on the root-seat)
The kept thread — settled at the base of her sternum, hum faint and steady
threads: What Caelum gave the Ashwardens in exchange for being taken in — unknown; Maren believes Aelyn will recognize it
The city's wrongness on the left root-wall — identified, not addressed, Maren does not know Aelyn found the site
The road northeast: Quickwood margin, Hollow March, Ashwarden lands — next step undecided

npcUpdates.statusNote: Standing in the entrance hall near the sapling, her evening post; she watched Aelyn leave without trying to add anything more.

codexUpdates.label: Caelum Kantoro
codexUpdates.fact: Caelum Kantoro — Aelyn's father, alive and northeast, almost certainly in the Ashwarden lands. He gave them something in return for being taken in; Maren believes Aelyn will recognize what it was. The kept thread settles and confirms his name when she writes it.

timeOps.why: A brief settling outside — a moment of thanks, sitting, writing in the journal before full dark.

quests.title: The Root That Remembers
quests.summary: The Root-That-Remembers beneath the Heartroot was waiting for Aelyn. A passage into the deep grain exists. Follow it — and answer.
progress: The kept thing has released its thread — a warm, deep-woven strand now settled in Aelyn at the base of her sternum. The thing that was held has been given. What it means, and who left it, remains.

The kept thing's thread has been traced: it points northeast to Aelyn's father, woven by someone who shared her deep-woven frequency. The Root's first need is answered. The second — the wrongness in the root-wall — is resolved. What remains: who left this thread, and why.

Maren has confirmed: the thread was left by Aelyn's father, thirty years ago. He was deep-woven, walked the deep grain, was heard back, and made the Heartroot promise to hold the thread for his daughter. She has his name.

Maren has clarified the northeast thread's nature: it is a living frequency that speaks only through movement. Aelyn holds it now. The direction is northeast; the destination will declare itself as she walks.
The kept thing has been received. Caelum Kantoro's name is known. Aelyn has thanked Maren and turned northeast — the thread hums at her sternum, orienting as she moves.
Aelyn has written the name, the thread, and Branch's word in her journal — the receiving is closed. She faces northeast. The wrongness in the city wall remains for another day.
Aelyn reached through Lifesense and confirmed the kept thread's orientation — northeast, steady, the other end a living person. Her father is findable. The first step of the path is confirmed.
The receiving is complete. Aelyn has written what she knows: Caelum Kantoro, alive, northeast, the Ashwarden lands. The thread at her sternum is steady. The path forward is named, not yet walked.

heartroot-tender.name: Rootkin Tender
heartroot-tender.role: Keeper of the entrance hall and the sapling-child within
heartroot-tender.description: A Rootkin of unhurried manner and deliberate attention — she has been watching Aelyn since arrival and has now decided something.
history: [d1] Stepped closer and spoke her first words after watching Aelyn use the Grove with the sapling — 'You speak to the young one.'
[d1] Revealed her decision plainly: Aelyn is not most visitors — she watched her listen first, not touch without invitation, and speak to the sapling through the Green rather than at it
knownFacts: She noticed Aelyn using the Grove with the sapling-child
She reached some quiet internal decision before approaching
She has been watching Aelyn since she entered and reached a deliberate conclusion
She recognized Aelyn's Grove-use as something rare — speaking through the Green, not merely to it
She gives her trust rarely and by her own measure, not by announcement
heartroot-tender.statusNote: Standing one step from Aelyn in the entrance hall, having asked her name and what the sapling said — waiting.

rootkin-tender.name: Maren
history: [d1] Known from what has passed between you.

[d1] Confirmed Caelum Kantoro said nothing of a destination when he left thirty years ago — only looked northeast and walked. Named what lies that way: past the Quickwood's margin and the Hollow March, the Ashwarden lands.

[d1] Named her belief plainly: Caelum went to the Ashwarden lands because the Rootkin would have pressed him to lean into the green, and the Ashwardens work with what a thing actually is. Doesn't know what he gave them in return but believes Aelyn will recognize it.
[d1] Aelyn thanked her plainly before stepping out — Maren accepted it with a small nod, fingers lifting briefly from the sapling and returning.
knownFacts: Caelum Kantoro left thirty years ago with no stated destination — he stood in the doorway, looked northeast, and walked
Maren was young when he left, not yet Tender, but she remembers
Northeast past the Quickwood's margin and the Hollow March lie the Ashwarden lands — the pole of tended endings
Caelum Kantoro went northeast to the Ashwarden lands — Maren's considered belief
The Ashwardens do not take people in out of kindness — if Caelum stayed, he gave them something
A deep-woven man carrying both sources would not be asked to simplify himself among the Ashwardens, as he would be among the Rootkin
rootkin-tender.statusNote: Standing in the entrance hall near the sapling, her evening post; she watched Aelyn leave without trying to add anything more.

notes: [d1] A passage into the deep grain was disclosed — grown four hundred years ago, located beneath or adjacent to the entrance hall. Aelyn now knows it exists.

[d1] The deep grain passage has been physically located — far end of the entrance hall, where the floor slopes downward and the moss thickens. The opening is grown heartwood, barely shoulder-width, framed
[d1] Aelyn crossed the threshold of the deep grain passage and began descending — the first time anyone has entered with the Root-That-Remembers' recognition.

[d1] A specific section of the left root-wall in the Root-That-Remembers' chamber has been identified by Branch as the site of the city's wrongness — visually indistinguishable from the surrounding wall.

[d1] The kept thing in the left root-wall of the Root-That-Remembers' chamber has woken to Aelyn's frequency — it is no longer dormant. The chamber has shifted in quality: something that was very still is
[d1] The kept thing in the left root-wall has settled into a new quality — no longer waiting for recognition, now waiting for Aelyn's first words. The chamber's quality has shifted perceptibly.
[d1] The seam of the kept thing's binding in the left root-wall has been opened — the fold has lifted and the kept thing stands ready to release what it holds.

[d1] The kept thing in the left root-wall of the Root-That-Remembers' chamber has fully released its thread — the seam is open and empty. The binding is resolved. The chamber's quality has shifted again: s

the-deep-grain-passage.name: The Deep Grain Passage
the-deep-grain-passage.note: A narrow corridor of pale heartwood and old roots, descending from the entrance hall's far end into warm amber dark. Mar

the-wrongness-site-left-root-wall-sect.note: The kept thing has been fully released. The seam is open. The root-wall is no longer holding anything — the thread is no

news.text: The Water Crisis stands at Discoloration across the valley: The Echo River runs gray-green on bad mornings. Fish catches are down. Communities murmur but cooperate.

This reaches your area — The lower filtration ledger — containing three weeks of falsified purity readings under Fendt's initials — has been removed from the array-office in the Edge District. (near radiant plateau edge)
Word reaches you: A dead animal was brought back to life in Millbrook's upper meadow — no known ability in the valley's three civilizations accounts for it cleanly (near millbrook)

Word reaches you: A death-binding ceremony was completed at Pell's forge in Millbrook — runes pressed into wood and iron with a maker's mark seated into the blade. The spear holds a nascent thread to Huginn and an open (near millbrook)
Word reaches you: Sow killed cleanly in the north wood — boar sounder now reduced; piglets scattered north into deeper scrub (near millbrook)
Word reaches you: Bull boar taken on the scrub oak ridge north of Millbrook — the hunt party completed the job, both animals down. (near millbrook)

This reaches your area — Fendt has left the Edge District and is walking to Millbrook — his testimony names Overseer Grael explicitly; when he arrives, the paper becomes a political event in both settlements. (near radiant plateau edge)
The Water Crisis stands at First Sickness across the valley: Upstream fisher families fall ill. Boiling water becomes universal. Harmonic and Radiant leaders trade public accusations.
This reaches your area — Two road opportunists at the limestone bend were disarmed and sent to Mara Wells in Millbrook for honest work — Silas Weir's name given as introduction (near millbrook)
Word reaches you: Compound rune-work — Wither and The Raised Thing — bound an eleven-year rising in the old Ashwarden warden post at Raven's Home. (near gen-ashwarden-march-road)
Rootkin Tender: The Tender has begun sleeping in the entrance hall rather than her usual chamber, unwilling to leave the sapling unobserved through the long nights.
Branch: The Cutting has moved closer — it was spotted briefly in a doorway one corridor nearer to where the player last was, then gone before it could be addressed.

Word reaches you: The first Ashwarden ending attended at the Raven's Home ground since its reclaiming — a plain death, witnessed without ceremony, in the hollow below the southeast birch line. (near gen-ashwarden-march-road)

Word reaches you: Silas Weir named Overseer Grael formally to Warden Coll as the receiving authority in the filtration accountability chain — on the record, in the Registry office, with a document roll in hand (near gen-north-gate-registry-ossian-office)

Word reaches you: A persistent shadow-slate bound to Silas Weir's Weirmark is now seated on Warden Coll's desk — a two-way written channel that does not require runners. (near gen-north-gate-registry-ossian-office)

Branch: The Cutting was found this time not in a doorway but crouched at the base of a wall two corridors nearer still, leaving shallow parallel marks in the stone before it withdrew — it is moving with purpose now, not wandering.

form: A young woman in her mid-twenties with light blonde, faintly wavy hair cut in curtain bangs, the rest falling to the small of her back — usually worn in a french braid with the bangs left loose. Dark nature-green tank top, brown heavy-material fitted trousers, and natural leather armor pieces worn light for speed. A recurve bow across her back, a dagger at her hip. Something in her eyes is very still and very watchful, like a forest that knows it is being observed.

bio.hometown: She lives in a secret safe haven in a pure nature forest, untouched by nanites. Her mother is an extremely powerful pure nature magic woman, and her father is a pure nanite that urged them to hide, and when nature no longer touched him, he fully succumbed to the nanites and is now an all powerful nanite, not remembering his love, nor his child (Aelyn)
bio.residence: In the hidden forest with her dying mother, and other pure nature people
bio.livelihood: They don't have a financial system
bio.hobbies: patrol, practice archery, make fairy houses, talk to Branch, climb through the trees, write in her journal
bio.motivation: Her mother dies, and she decides to go to the nanite world, to figure out why she is different than the pure nature people.
bio.story:
Aelyn is a young woman in her mid twenties, born of both the worlds of pure nature (her mother) and of the world of pure nanites (her father). she has light blonde, ever so slightly wavy hair, with curtain bangs and the rest of the hair falling to the small of her back. she often wears it in a french braid with her bangs left out. she lives in a secluded, pure nature, hidden refuge in a lush beautiful forest, hidden from the nanites to protect the nature's natural magic. her mother has told her that her father died due to nanite corruption, when in reality he is still alive as an angelic nanite, that has become corrupted by the fabricated power of technology. They loved each other so much, and her father was actually the one who told the pure nature people to hide themselves from the nanites, so their magic wouldn't die. But after they left and he was no longer touched by the magic of nature, the nanites fully corrupted him because it was evolving too much, too fast. Her mother grieved him, in a way even though he didn't die, which greatly hindered her powers, locking them away in a way. 

Aelyn is an expert archer. She is also handy with a dagger and other bladed weapons when necessary. She wears a base outfit of a dark, nature green tank top and tighter, brown slightly thicker material jeans. She has natural crafted leather armor pieces that allow for basic protection but to keep agility to use her bow. She is extremely agile, able to maneuver through trees and all nature foliage with ease and expertise. She has some outside knowledge of the nanite world, keeping up with this small protected civilization's patrol reports. 

Her heritage being of both innate worlds is hidden because she has never left pure nature, so her nanite aspects have been what would be considered dormant. Both her mother and her father are extremely powerful in their respective worlds.  

Aelyn is a grounded, smart woman who is extremely empathetic, but guarded. She isn't scared of the outside world, but rather intimidated by how it consumes the natural world. She craves balance, but also stands by her morals and what's right. Her mother is dying, and she feels lost, unsure of what to do without her. She possesses both great natural, nature magic, and also a hidden, vast nanite power. she has yet to discover both.

heartroot.label: The Heartroot
facts: [d1] A city grown over four centuries by the Rootkin — living wood halls, breathing ceilings, floors of old moss. The Rootkin who planted it are still alive and consider it unfinished.

[d1] Lifesense read something warm and distributed moving through the hall's grain — slow as sap, not quite alive in any recognizable way, but not absent of vitality either. It diffused before Aelyn could

[d1] The Heartroot grew around something that predates it — older than the hall, older than the Rootkin who planted the first seed. The sapling knows it as ordinary. It is not the walls themselves, but the

[d1] The entrance hall was deliberately planted above the deep grain — not for proximity to the Root-That-Remembers, but as an act of worthiness. The city's oldest roots predate both the city and the Rootk

[d1] Located at the far end of the entrance hall where the floor slopes downward and the moss thickens. A grown opening of pale heartwood, barely shoulder-width, descending into the deep grain. Old roots l
[d1] Aelyn crossed the threshold and descended. The passage is warmer than the hall above, narrower than expected, the enormous root-walls smooth with age. Maren did not follow. Branch did.

[d1] The Root-That-Remembers holds a part of itself that is not-well — not failing, not broken, but wrong and unnamed. Branch sensed it too, attending the left root-wall in the chamber. The city has not sp

[d1] The city's wrongness has a precise location: a section of the left root-wall deep in the Root-That-Remembers' chamber, identified by Branch through root-contact and bud-sense. It looks no different fr

[d1] Prism Sight cannot read the wrongness site — the Heartroot's sourceless ambient light gives the craft no gradient, no shadow, no heat-trace to parse. The wrongness must be approached through a differe

[d1] Lifesense confirms the wrongness is not sickness or damage — the life-current in that section of root-wall is stalled, pooled, held. It reads like a voice in a choir that has dropped to a murmur: stil

[d1] The Heartroot has a word for the wrongness in the root-wall: 'kept.' Not sickness, not damage — something deliberate holds a thread of the life-current folded aside and left there. The word implies an

[d1] The kept thing was a thread woven by someone who shared Aelyn's deep-woven frequency — green-and-other, doubled. Its shaped impression points northeast: her father, findable, alive. Someone knew that
[d1] It was placed by Aelyn's father thirty years ago — he asked the Heartroot to hold it for his daughter. The Heartroot agreed and kept its promise across three decades.
[d?] you have walked here
aliases: The Heartroot — Something in the Wood
The Unnamed Wrongness in the Heartroot

echo-river-discoloration.label: The Echo River — Gray-Green
facts: [d1] The Echo River runs gray-green on bad mornings. Fish catches are down. Aelyn saw it herself when she forded it yesterday.

branch.label: Branch
facts: [d1] In the Heartroot's entrance hall, Branch fixed all five buds open on the sapling-child and spoke its first deliberate words here: 'Old. Very. Old. And. Watching.' — a warning or a recognition, not yet

[d1] Branch felt the Root-That-Remembers attending through the floor — its buds tipped down in deliberate stillness, the Grove confirmed its awareness was oriented the same direction as Aelyn's Lifesense.
[d1] Spoke directly to Maren — a Rootkin elder — without prompting: 'Down. Old. Watching. Us.' Its first deliberate speech addressed to someone other than Aelyn.
[d1] Spoke a single deliberate word — 'Down' — into the silence after Maren finished speaking. Its buds remained fully open and motionless. It knew before Maren finished.

[d1] Felt the Root-That-Remembers' partial answer at the same moment Aelyn did — made a drawn-out creak and tilted its buds sideways, as if listening for the rest of a direction. It may have received a dif

[d1] Made a short, almost sharp creak when the Root-That-Remembers confirmed Aelyn's dual nature — buds turned fully upward then stopped. It seems to have received its own message in the same exchange.
[d1] Branch attended the left root-wall in the chamber and confirmed 'wrong' — twice, deliberately — with a directional bud-tilt. It senses a location for the city's unnamed wrongness.
[d1] Branch confirmed root-to-root that the kept thing is still present and waiting — not merely the shape of where something was. It handled 'waiting' as a new word, with care.

[d1] Branch felt the kept thread's release independently — it oriented northeast before Aelyn moved, and named the frequency as 'old — the same old as me' and 'family.' It can sense kin-frequency through r
[d?] The Cutting — Small, silent, following. Watching everything. Has not spoken yet.
[d1] [while away] The Cutting has moved closer — it was spotted briefly in a doorway one corridor nearer to where the player last was, then gone before it could be addressed.

[d1] [while away] The Cutting was found this time not in a doorway but crouched at the base of a wall two corridors nearer still, leaving shallow parallel marks in the stone before it withdrew — it is moving with purpose now, not wandering.

the-watching-thing.label: The Watching Presence
facts: [d1] Something ancient occupies the heart of the Heartroot — predating the city, perhaps predating the Rootkin who built it. It watches. The sapling senses it without fear. The Grove confirmed its presence
[d1] Its center lies below the entrance hall — down through the root-floor into the Heartroot's deepest grain. The sapling gave the direction clearly: inward, below, where the oldest roots run.

sapling-child-heartroot.label: The Sapling-Child of the Heartroot
facts: [d1] The sapling acknowledged Aelyn's greeting with a deliberate dip of its raised branch — it was waiting to be seen. Its warmth through Lifesense brightened briefly in response.
[d1] [while away] The sapling stands exactly as it was — branch still half-raised, warmth steady but unspent, still waiting for whoever comes next to see it properly.
[d1] [while away] The sapling stands exactly as before — branch half-raised, warmth steady and unspent, still waiting; another day has passed through it like light through a held breath.

rootkin-tender.label: Maren
facts: [d1] Her name is Maren. She gave it in exchange for Aelyn's, with the gravity of a handshake. She is the keeper of the entrance hall. She recognized Branch immediately as Rootkin kin and marked it as signi
[d1] Maren's memory is long — she said so plainly. She knows the Rootkin who walked the deep grain, and she knows none of them went down to be heard.
[d1] Keeper of the entrance hall and the sapling-child within
[d1] met in play
[d1] [while away] The Tender has begun sleeping in the entrance hall rather than her usual chamber, unwilling to leave the sapling unobserved through the long nights.

[d1] [while away] The Tender has kept her post without incident, tending the entrance hall and checking on the sapling at intervals, but nothing has shifted in her watch — the day passed in quiet, repetitive vigilance.

the-watching-presence.label: The Root-That-Remembers
facts: [d1] The Rootkin call it the Root-That-Remembers — or some do; others refuse to name it at all. It predates the hall and the Rootkin who planted it. They grew the city around it deliberately, the way you'd

[d1] Lifesense reached it through the root-floor: distributed warmth, slow as stone-held heat, with a quality of oriented attention — not sleep, not speech, but awareness. It registered this conversation t
[d1] Maren confirmed: the Root-That-Remembers has not made itself known to a visitor in a long time — not to anyone who came through the entrance. Its attention toward this conversation is unusual.

[d1] The Rootkin grow the city around it deliberately — like a city around a well. They tend the distance. Older Rootkin walk the deep grain to listen; no one, in Maren's memory, has gone down to be heard

[d1] The deep grain runs directly below the entrance hall — the Rootkin planted the first hall above this spot not to be near it, but to be worthy of it. The roots run deeper than the Rootkin have ever ful

[d1] After the sapling's direction was spoken aloud, the Root-That-Remembers shifted its attention — no longer the whole room, but Aelyn specifically. It recognized something in her, and is now listening f

[d1] The recognition runs both ways. When Aelyn followed the Root's attending attention back to its source, the Root responded — not with words but with the quality of something that has been waiting. Not

[d1] The Rootkin named it not from understanding but from observation — something was already here when they planted the first hall, its roots going deeper than theirs ever followed. It chose this soil bef

[d1] A passage into the deep grain exists — grown by the Rootkin four hundred years ago. No Rootkin who walked it ever heard anything back. The Root-That-Remembers was not listening to them. It was listeni

[d1] The passage into the deep grain is alive — old roots framing it, slow-pulsed, with the Root-That-Remembers as a distributed field below. Nothing hostile. It waits with total certainty that Aelyn will

[d1] When Aelyn spoke her presence into the passage — plain words, not ability-use — the slow pulse in the root-walls deepened by a beat and the amber light below sharpened. The Root-That-Remembers heard h

[d1] When asked directly what it wants Aelyn to know, it answered in pressure rather than words: old, many, and Aelyn as a point of convergence it has been tracing through the soil-network for a very long

[d1] When asked directly whether it knows both sides of Aelyn's nature — her mother's green and her father's nanite inheritance — it answered yes, both, known together. It holds the two threads as already

[d1] The Root-That-Remembers knows Aelyn's father — not just the thread of him in her, but him, what he became. It holds both ends of the root: one buried in the deep green, one pointing outward into the v

[d1] When asked whether Aelyn's father is findable, the Root answered: yes, but partially. The thread points northeast — real, large in the way the Root measures large things, distant in the way old roots

[d1] Aelyn entered the chamber in silence — and the Root's attention encompassed her immediately, from the inside, the way something very large includes a new part of itself. Not a greeting; an already-kno

[d1] The Root-That-Remembers read Aelyn fully through the Grove — both the green from her mother and the structural thread from her father, held simultaneously, not apart. It has finished its question and

[d1] When Aelyn asked what the Root needed from her, it answered with two things: the northeast thread (her father, findable, distant) and a local wrongness inside the city — something not-well in a way th
[d1] The kept thing in the root-wall is confirmed still present — alive at a low note, folded aside, and waiting. Branch used the word 'waiting' deliberately. It is not a residue.

[d1] The kept thing in the root-wall answered Aelyn's Grove-spoken question — something Maren says has never happened before for anyone who walked the deep grain. The Root-That-Remembers has been waiting f

aelyn-inner-frequency.label: The Frequency Beneath the Grove
facts: [d1] Lifesense turned inward revealed a resonance in Aelyn that predates her ability — a frequency the Root-That-Remembers recognized before she did. It is not the Grove itself but the register underneath

[d1] Maren gave Aelyn's inward frequency a name: the deep-woven. It is older than the Grove and not learned — it is something Aelyn was born carrying, recognized by the Root-That-Remembers before she reach

[d1] The Root-That-Remembers does not simply recognize the frequency Aelyn carries — it knows it, the way a root knows its specific water-table: intimately, by absence and presence both. Aelyn was expected
[d1] On entering the chamber, the deep-woven frequency stirred faintly — like a string touched by a passing hand. Not sound. Recognition. It responded to being inside the Root-That-Remembers.

[d1] When Aelyn opened through the Grove and made herself legible, the deep-woven frequency was read by the Root-That-Remembers — both its green aspect and its other, structural aspect recognized and held

[d1] The older structural frequency in Aelyn was what the kept thing paused on — it recognized the green first, then found the other hum and was not surprised. It had been waiting for exactly this combined
[d1] The thread the kept thing released is the same frequency as Aelyn's deep-woven hum — and it was left here deliberately by someone who knew that frequency, before Aelyn was born.

[d1] The kept thread's orientation confirmed through Lifesense — northeast, steady, the weight of a living person. Caelum Kantoro is alive and the thread reaches him clearly, past the limit of Lifesense's range.

the-deep-woven.label: The Deep-Woven
facts: [d1] A Rootkin word for a person in whom the green and something older run together undivided at the source — not two separate things learned, but one thing whole. The Rootkin thought it was legend. Maren

[d1] The Rootkin legend of the deep-woven assumed a single source root — green at the origin. Maren admitted this assumption may have been too narrow. The Root-That-Remembers recognized Aelyn before her he

[d1] Branch confirmed: what is held in the wrongness section of the root-wall was once someone — 'something that was someone. Old.' The keeping is deliberate, and the kept thing retains a person-shape.
[d1] Aelyn's father was also deep-woven — the Root knew him and heard him back. The Rootkin believed it legend; it has now happened twice, father and daughter.

aelyn-father.label: Aelyn's Father
facts: [d1] The Root-That-Remembers confirmed he exists and is known to the deep grain — bright and immense and no longer soft, the nanite thread pointing somewhere out in the valley. The Root holds the connectio

[d1] The kept thing's thread confirmed he is alive — northeast, findable, at a real distance. The thread was woven by someone with the same deep-woven frequency, implying someone besides Aelyn's parents kn

[d1] He came to the Heartroot approximately thirty years ago — deep-woven, known by the Root. He walked the deep grain and was heard back — the only time before Aelyn. He left the thread in the root-wall d

[d1] His name is Caelum Kantoro. He sat two days in the Heartroot's entrance hall before he spoke. The Root knew his frequency the moment he arrived. He descended the deep grain, was heard back, and came b
[d1] Aelyn has turned northeast to find him — the thread at her sternum hums and orients as she moves. The direction will declare itself as she walks.
[d1] His name is written now — the first concrete thing Aelyn has set down about him in her own hand. The thread points northeast. A direction is becoming a destination.
[d1] Left the Heartroot thirty years ago with no stated destination — stood in the entrance doorway, looked northeast toward the Pale, and walked. Maren was young then and remembers it.

[d1] Maren believes Caelum went to the Ashwarden lands because the Rootkin would have pressed him to lean into the green — the Ashwardens work with what a thing actually is, and would not have tried to simplify his deep-woven nature. He gave them something in return for being taken in; Maren doesn't…

[d1] Caelum Kantoro — Aelyn's father, alive and northeast, almost certainly in the Ashwarden lands. He gave them something in return for being taken in; Maren believes Aelyn will recognize what it was. The kept thread settles and confirms his name when she writes it.

the-wrongness-site.label: The Wrongness Site — 'Kept'
facts: [d1] Branch gave the Heartroot's word: 'kept.' The life-current in this section of root-wall is not broken or sick — it is being held deliberately. Something or someone folded it aside and left it there.

the-kept-thing.label: The Kept Thing
facts: [d1] The kept thing is not trapped. It has been patient — waiting for permission from someone it recognizes. The frequency it knows is Aelyn's own. When Aelyn spoke through the Grove and asked what it wait

[d1] The kept thing has fully recognized Aelyn's dual frequency — the green and the older structural hum beneath it. It is now awake and attending within the root-wall. It confirmed her as the specific per

[d1] The kept thing completed its reading of Aelyn's full frequency — both the green and the older structural hum — and settled into a new kind of waiting. Not for permission, not for recognition: for what

[d1] The kept thing answered Aelyn's question with two things: the seam of its own binding (a precise, deliberate fold that her frequency can undo — it showed her how, like a key and a lock-shape behind th
[d1] Aelyn found the seam of the binding by feel — not a knot but a cupped hand, holding something. The fold opened to her older structural frequency. Something is ready to be given.

[d1] The kept thing was a thread — warm, deep-woven, held at the same frequency as Aelyn's own hidden hum. It has been received and now hums at the base of Aelyn's sternum. Someone wove it in and left it t

the-northeast-thread.label: The Northeast Thread
facts: [d1] The thread points to a living frequency — Caelum's, or something carrying his signature. It cannot give distance, only direction, and it will speak only when Aelyn is moving. She already holds what he

harrow-the-hollowing.label: Harrow, Who Makes People Into Mechanisms
facts: [d1] [while away] No visible movement yet — Harrow's designs are slow and vast, and five days have left only the same distant silence around her name.

[d1] [while away] Harrow's name surfaces once in a piece of overheard corridor talk — someone who passed through her territory three weeks ago, describing a gate that had been recently re-hinged — but nothing more.

the-hollow-march.label: The Hollow March
facts: [d1] A stretch northeast of the Quickwood's margin where growth simply stops — the border-land before the Ashwarden pole. Maren names it as what lies between the Rootkin lands and the Pale.

ashwarden-lands.label: The Ashwarden Lands (The Pale)
facts: [d1] The pole of endings, northeast past the Hollow March. The Rootkin do not go there. The kept thread leads in that direction — and Caelum Kantoro walked that way thirty years ago with no stated destination.

ashwarden.label: The Ashwardens
facts: [d1] The Ashwardens work with what a thing actually is — they do not press a person to simplify themselves. They do not take people in out of kindness; if someone stays among them, they give something in return. The Ashwarden lands lie northeast past the Quickwood's margin and the Hollow March.

establishedFacts.text: Aelyn's mother has died. Aelyn left the hidden refuge and entered the wider valley for the first time.
The watching presence is centered below the Heartroot's entrance hall — down through the root-floor and the deepest grain, where the city's oldest roots run. The sapling gave Aelyn the direction.
The Root-That-Remembers is awake and attending — Lifesense confirmed distributed, slow awareness oriented toward the conversation above it.

The deep grain beneath the entrance hall predates the Rootkin and the city itself — its roots run deeper than anyone has mapped. The Rootkin planted the first hall above it not to be near it but to be
Aelyn turned Lifesense inward and found a frequency in herself — older than her Grove-craft — that hums at the same register as the Root-That-Remembers. She does not yet know its origin.
Maren named Aelyn 'deep-woven' — a Rootkin concept for a person carrying both the green and something older at the source, undivided. The Rootkin believed it to be legend until this moment.

Maren acknowledged the Rootkin understanding of 'deep-woven' may have been too narrow — they assumed one source root only. Aelyn's dual heritage (green and not-green) has not caused the Root-That-Reme

A passage into the deep grain exists beneath the Heartroot — grown by the Rootkin four hundred years ago. No one who has walked it has ever heard anything back. Maren has disclosed its existence to Ae

The Root-That-Remembers has two needs it communicated to Aelyn: the northeast thread (her father, findable and real) and something wrong within the city itself — unnamed, not broken, but wrong in a wa
The kept thing in the root-wall of the Root-That-Remembers' chamber is confirmed still present — alive, held at a low note, and waiting. Branch confirmed this root-to-root.

The kept thing in the root-wall is waiting for permission — it is not trapped and not restless. It has been patient for a very long time. What it waits for is a frequency it already knows: the one Ael

The kept thing in the root-wall is now fully awake and attending — it has recognized Aelyn's complete frequency (both the green and the older structural hum) and confirmed her as the one it was waitin
Aelyn has found and opened the seam of the kept thing's binding in the left root-wall — the fold has lifted and the kept thing is ready to release what it holds.
The kept thing in the root-wall of the Root-That-Remembers' chamber has fully released its thread to Aelyn — a warm, deep-woven strand settled now at the base of her sternum. The binding is resolved.

Aelyn's father's name is Caelum Kantoro. He visited the Heartroot thirty years ago, sat in the entrance hall for two days listening, descended the deep grain passage, was heard by the Root-That-Rememb
Aelyn has left the Heartroot with the kept thread settled in her sternum, Caelum Kantoro's name known, and the thread orienting northeast as she moves. The deep grain quest's receiving is complete.

chronicleCache.hash: d0|b|c|q|L1|Her mother dies, and she decides to go to the na
chronicleCache.text: You have become someone whose grief is still raw enough to taste — your mother barely cold in the earth when you packed what little you owned and walked away from the only life you knew among the Rootkin, the people of pure nature, the ones who never needed to ask what they were. But you have always needed to ask. The nanite-laced difference in you has been a quiet wrongness your whole life, a hum beneath the skin that no root or leaf could explain, and now, with no mother left to avoid the question for, you are walking toward the answer. You know no one yet. You have done nothing yet. You are only beginning.

sessions.recap: Day seventeen passed without incident, and that itself was the event. You are Aelyn Kantoro, and you spent the day in the strange suspended quiet of someone waiting for a life to begin — no errand urgent enough to pull you out the door, no voice calling your name with anything that mattered. The stillness settled into you the way dust settles into a room no one has crossed in hours: not unpleasant, just present, just real. Something is coming. You can feel the shape of it without seeing the face.

The twenty-fourth day passed without fanfare — no blade drawn, no threshold crossed, no name added to the ledger of the dead or the saved. Aelyn moved through the world quietly, and the world moved around her in kind, the hours folding into one another with the particular texture of stillness that comes not from absence but from waiting. Whatever is gathering at the edges of her story has not yet shown its face, and she remains: present, unspent, ready.

personalArc.name: The Braid Between Worlds
personalArc.premise: A young woman raised in pure nature's silence — her father's name a grave her mother never stopped visiting — steps into the nanite world carrying both its blood and its opposite, and finds the thing her mother called dead is still reaching, still searching, still almost her father.
personalArc.stakes: To reclaim the man inside the machine, to end what he became, or to refuse both answers and become the living proof that the braid holds — the thing neither world could make alone.

legendNpc.name: Caelum Kantoro
legendNpc.role: the fallen Seraph, her father, the man who loved her mother enough to hide her from himself — and lost himself in the si

stages.objective: the last word she kept — her mother's breath goes out in The Heartroot, and with it the seal on everything she was never told: the name of a man who didn't die, and the grief that was always a leash

the dormant signature — as Aelyn crosses from pure nature into the world her father built and then surrendered to, something in her blood wakes: her nanite aspects flicker to life in the substrate lik

the anomaly and the daughter — Caelum arrives, all light and architecture and the terrible beauty of a man who has been algorithm for so long he no longer knows what he is mourning; and Aelyn stands a

personalArc.notes_for_gm: A generated PERSONAL arc (SNG-133) — surface it as a slow gravity across sessions; the ending is the player's, never foreclosed.

entries.why: a hand in this people's craft
the far side of the circle from your own

standingLedger.text: Your birth and training are known: rootkin +4, seraphic +2, verist +1, ashwarden -1.
